words.definition: to collect and combine data from multiple sources into a single set or total
words.example: The dashboard aggregates metrics from every microservice into one view.

words.definition: to estimate unknown values by extending a known pattern or trend
words.example: Analysts extrapolated next quarter's growth from the last six months of data.

words.definition: showing a very fine level of detail
words.example: We need more granular logging to pinpoint exactly where the request failed.
words.usage_note: Common in tech/data contexts, less so in everyday speech.

words.definition: something that deviates from what is standard or expected
words.example: The monitoring system flagged an anomaly in overnight traffic patterns.

words.definition: to show or find a mutual relationship between two or more things
words.example: Higher latency correlated strongly with a recent spike in database load.

words.definition: to express or measure something as a number or amount
words.example: It's hard to quantify exactly how much the redesign improved retention.

words.definition: to distort data or results so they no longer represent an accurate picture
words.example: A few extreme outliers skewed the average response time.

words.definition: to measure the performance of something against a standard or comparison point
words.example: We benchmarked the new algorithm against the old one on the same dataset.

words.definition: a difference or inconsistency between two things that should match
words.example: There's a discrepancy between the logs and the actual database records.

words.definition: to confirm or support a claim or finding with additional evidence
words.example: The second dataset corroborated our initial hypothesis about user churn.
words.usage_note: Formal/academic register; rare in casual speech.
